name: [M] flexible-days
description:
Flexible date-range search. Replace fixed (weekday,nights) pattern with SPAN + min/max nights; enumerate sub-windows where min<=nights<=max.

DECISIONS:
- Syntax: per-pattern suffix 'fri-mon:2-3' (span:min-max). Bare 'fri-mon' => min=max=span_nights.
- Backward compat: existing 'fri-sun' unchanged => single 2n window (old behavior).
- Dedup: prefer LONGEST stay per start date (generalize current 1n/2n suppression).
- Explosion: no hard cap; warn/log when a pattern expands to many windows.

Example: span FRI-MON min2 max3 => FRI-SUN(2n), SAT-MON(2n), FRI-MON(3n).

Touches: profile.py (parse_pattern + model), search.py (expand_windows enumeration + prefer-longest dedup), tests.
Grilled: yes
result: Flexible date-range search implemented: parse_pattern min-max suffix, expand_windows enumeration, prefer-longest dedup, backward-compat integration. 6 commits landed. All existing tests pass.

name: [S] expand_windows enumeration
description: search.py: enumerate windows for ranged patterns. For each pattern's start_weekday d in horizon, for o in 0..(span_nights-min_nights), for n in min..max s.t. o+n <= span_nights, emit (start=d+o, nights=n). Keep past-date, min_start, max_start guards. Worked example FRI-MON:2-3 -> FRI-SUN(2n), SAT-MON(2n), FRI-MON(3n). Tests: TestExpandWindows asserts exact emitted set for ranged pattern; bare-pattern regression lock (no suffix -> identical to today); min_start/max_start/past-date guards still filter emitted starts. Parent PRD: mq9caivh.
result: Replaced TODO placeholder in src/campcli/application/search.py::expand_windows with full offset×length enumeration per PRD: for each pattern's anchor d, iterate o in [0, span-min] and n in [min, max] with constraint o+n <= span_nights. Guards (today, min_start, max_start) apply to the emitted start date, not the anchor. Worked example (fri-mon:2-3 anchored Fri) emits (Fri,2), (Sat,2), (Fri,3) — o=1,n=3 correctly rejected. Bare pattern regression-locked: fri-sun still emits (Fri,2) only. 6 new TestExpandWindows cases in tests/test_search.py covering: ranged pattern exact-set, bare regression, min_start on emitted start, max_start on emitted start, past-date anchor, min==max==span. Verification: 162 passed, 0 failed, 0 warnings, 0 xfailed in 1.30s (/home/andre/git/campcli/.venv/bin/pytest). name: [M] mark-not-interested
description:
Let a profile mark a specific park+date-range suggestion as NotInterested so it is never suggested again.

PRD: .scratch/mark-not-interested/PRD.md

## Design decisions (grilled)

- Domain concept: `NotInterested` — scoped to (profile, park_id, date_start, date_end).
- Persistence: `profile_not_interested` table (profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end) FK → profiles.
- Telegram: reply /not-interested to a notification message. No bot command registration.
- Message tracking: `sent_notifications` table records (message_id, profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end) at send time. Looked up on /not-interested via `reply_to_message_id`.
- TelegramUpdate gains `reply_to_message_id: int | None` field, parsed in poll_updates().
- send_to() returns message_id (int) for tracking.
- SearchNotifier receives NotInterestedRepo protocol for filtering and sent-message recording.
- Filtering: SearchNotifier.notify() skips matches whose (park_id, start_date, end_date) matches a NotInterested row loaded at poll start.
- CLI: `profile not-interested add/rm/list <name>` — requires park name, date_start, date_end. Park name resolved via API.
- Wave 2 (future): pre-filter exploded windows before API call. Not worth it yet.
- Thumbs-down reaction: captured as separate [S] task (hxtd9dzq).

Grilled: yes
result: Three slices landed on multi-profile: core repo (c615abc), telegram handler (417738c), CLI commands (2e9faed). Copy alignment follow-up (503b79c). 265/265 pytest pass. Master review (reviewer-strong) signed off PASS — no PRD drift, no integration gaps, architectural seams clean. PRD .scratch/mark-not-interested/PRD.md fully satisfied: profile-scoped skip-set with O(1) lookup, /not-interested reply-only Telegram handler, profile not-interested add/rm/list CLI, CASCADE delete, 90-day sent_notifications TTL on daemon startup.

archived_children.description:
## Parent
[M] mark-not-interested

## What to build
Add the /not-interested Telegram reply command. Extend TelegramUpdate with reply_to_message_id, parse it in poll_updates(), handle the command in command_router.dispatch(), and add a footer to notification messages teaching users about the command.

Handler flow:
1. Extract reply_to_message_id from TelegramUpdate
2. Look up sent_notifications via NotInterestedRepo.lookup_sent()
3. If no match → reply 'Could not find the notification for this message.'
4. Verify from_id is in profile's tg_allowed_ids
5. Insert into profile_not_interested
6. Reply confirming: 'NotInterested recorded: <Park> <dates>'

Standalone /not-interested (no reply context) → 'Reply this command to a notification message.'

/not-interested is NOT registered as a bot command (reply-only).

render_match_message() adds footer: 'Reply /not-interested to stop seeing this park for these dates.'

Make CLI search and check commands profile-aware. Add --profile option to search and check commands. Implement default profile logic: when exactly one enabled profile exists, omit --profile requirement. Add profile search <name> command for one-off profile-specific manual searches.

- campcli search (no --profile, 0 or 2+ enabled profiles): error with guidance

- [ ] campcli check --profile <name> works on the check command

- [ ] No enabled profiles → error: 'no enabled profiles found'
- [ ] Multiple enabled profiles → error: 'specify --profile'
- [ ] search command uses profile's allowed parks, patterns, horizon, drive-time
- [ ] Integration tests for each case above

name: [M] multi-profile-poller-and-migration
description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Refactor the Poller and daemon to support multiple profiles with crawl dedup. Add profile.json → DB migration.

Poller refactor:
- Load all enabled profiles via ProfileRepo.list_enabled()
- Collect unique park_id, map_id pairs across all profiles (resolve park/map queries against BC Parks catalog at start of each poll cycle)
- For each unique pair, sequentially: call BCParksApi.map_availability() once, then for each profile watching this park: run pattern explosion + availability matching + prefer-longest dedup. If matches found, notify via Telegram immediately (per-profile tg_allowed_ids), then proceed to next profile
- After all profiles for this park are processed, proceed to next park
- SearchNotifier is created per profile (each gets its own rest_days and drive_times config)

Daemon refactor:
- daemon.py loads ProfileRepo and passes list of enabled profiles to Poller
- Removes load_profile() call (no more profile.json)

Migration:
- On Poller startup (or first profile command), if profile.json exists and no profiles in DB: read profile.json, create a profile named 'default' with all fields populated from the JSON, delete profile.json
- This is a function called at daemon startup and before any profile command

The old application/profile.py load_profile and _make_default_profile are removed.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Poller.run_search_once() uses ProfileRepo.list_enabled() instead of a single Profile
- [ ] Unique park grouping: 2 profiles watching same park → API called once
- [ ] Sequential processing: park A → notify profiles → park B
- [ ] Per-profile notification: each profile's tg_allowed_ids receives only its matches
- [ ] SearchNotifier created per profile with profile-specific rest_days and drive_times
- [ ] Daemon loads profiles from ProfileRepo, not profile.json
- [ ] profile.json migration: exists + no DB profiles → create 'default' profile, delete JSON
- [ ] Migration called on daemon startup
- [ ] Old load_profile / _make_default_profile removed
- [ ] Unit tests: 2 profiles same park (dedup), 2 profiles different parks (no dedup), disabled profile skipped, empty profile list
- [ ] Integration test: daemon starts, migrates profile.json, polls correctly

name: PRD: Flexible-Days Search
description:
# PRD: Flexible-Days Search

## Problem Statement

Today I can only tell campcli to search for a stay that begins and ends on
fixed weekdays — e.g. `fri-sun` means "arrive Friday, leave Sunday, exactly two
nights." If I would also accept a Saturday arrival, or a three-night stay over
the same long weekend, I have to add several separate patterns and reason about
each one by hand. There is no way to say "any 2-to-3-night stay somewhere
inside this Friday-to-Monday window," which is how I actually think about a
camping trip.

## Solution

Let a pattern describe a **span** of days plus a **minimum and maximum number of
nights**, and have the search enumerate every stay that fits inside the span
whose length falls in that range.

A pattern gains an optional suffix: `fri-mon:2-3` means "inside the Friday →
Monday span, find any stay of 2 or 3 nights." That expands to:

- `fri-sun` (2 nights)
- `sat-mon` (2 nights)
- `fri-mon` (3 nights)

A bare pattern keeps working exactly as before: `fri-sun` still means a single
two-night Friday→Sunday stay. So the old behaviour is just the special case
where min = max = the span's own length, and existing `profile.json` files need
no changes.

## User Stories

1. As a camper, I want to write `fri-mon:2-3`, so that the search finds every 2-
   or 3-night stay inside the Friday-to-Monday window in one pattern.
2. As a camper, I want a bare pattern like `fri-sun` to keep meaning a fixed
   two-night stay, so that my existing profile keeps working unchanged.
3. As a camper, I want to set min = max (e.g. `fri-sun:2-2`), so that I can pin a
   span to exactly one stay length when I want the old strictness explicitly.
4. As a camper, I want min < max to widen the stay lengths, so that I can catch
   both a short weekend and a longer one with a single line.
5. As a camper, I want the span to be able to differ from the nights, so that
   `fri-mon:2-3` can start on Friday OR Saturday, not only on the span's first
   day.
6. As a camper, I want each enumerated sub-window checked against availability
   independently, so that a site free only for the Saturday-arrival stay still
   surfaces.
7. As a camper, when the same arrival date has both a shorter and a longer
   available stay, I want to be told about the longest one only, so that I am
   not spammed with overlapping options for the same night.
8. As a camper, I want an obviously broken pattern (unknown day, end-before-
   start, min > max, min < 1) to fail loudly when the profile loads, so that I
   fix the typo before a search runs.
9. As a camper, I want a warning when a pattern expands into a large number of
   windows, so that I notice an accidentally huge span before it multiplies my
   API traffic.
10. As a camper, I want the booking URL, the notification, and the match's
    night count to all agree for each enumerated stay, so that what I click
    matches what I was told.
11. As a camper, I want the existing horizon, drive-time, allowlist, and
    rest-day filters to apply unchanged to every enumerated window, so that
    flexibility does not bypass my other constraints.
12. As a camper mixing styles, I want to list a bare pattern and a ranged
    pattern together (`["fri-sun", "fri-mon:2-3"]`), so that I can migrate one
    pattern at a time.
13. As a developer, I want the span-and-range model to live behind the existing
    `parse_pattern` / `expand_windows` seams, so that the change is unit-testable
    without new infrastructure.

## Implementation Decisions

### Pattern syntax — per-pattern suffix

A pattern string is `span` or `span:min-max`:

- `span` is the existing `startday-endday` form (e.g. `fri-mon`). The span's
  length in nights is `end_weekday - start_weekday` (no wrap-around, no
  same-day), as today.
- `:min-max` is an optional suffix giving the inclusive night-count range.
- A bare span is equivalent to `span:N-N` where `N` is the span's own nights.
  This is what preserves backward compatibility.

Validation, all at profile-load time (fail-fast, same place patterns are
already validated): unknown day name; end ≤ start; `min < 1`; `min > max`;
`max` greater than the span length (a stay can't be longer than its window).

### `parse_pattern` return shape

`parse_pattern` currently returns `(start_weekday, nights)`. It will return a
richer structure carrying `(start_weekday, span_nights, min_nights,
max_nights)` (exact container — named tuple / dataclass — is an implementation
choice for planning). The bare-pattern case sets `min = max = span_nights`.
`Profile.pattern_tuples()` (or a renamed successor) returns the list of these.

### Window enumeration in `expand_windows`

For each calendar date `d` in the horizon that equals a pattern's
`start_weekday`, enumerate every offset `o` in `0 .. (span_nights - min_nights)`
and every length `n` in `min_nights .. max_nights` such that the stay starting
at `d + o` of length `n` still ends on or before the span's end (`o + n <=
span_nights`). Emit `(start_date = d + o, nights = n)`. The existing past-date,
`min_start`, and `max_start` guards continue to apply to the **emitted start
date**.

Worked example, span Fri→Mon (`span_nights = 3`), min 2 max 3, anchored on a
Friday `d`:

- `o=0, n=2` → Fri→Sun (2n)
- `o=1, n=2` → Sat→Mon (2n)
- `o=0, n=3` → Fri→Mon (3n)
- `o=1, n=3` → rejected (`1 + 3 > 3`)

### Prefer-longest dedup (generalised)

The current search loop has a special case that suppresses a 1-night stay when
a 2-night stay covers the same or adjacent start. Generalise it: process each
map's candidate windows sorted by start date then by descending nights; track
which `(start_date)` nights are already covered by an accepted longer stay, and
skip a shorter window whose nights are wholly contained in an already-accepted
stay from the same or an earlier overlapping start. The intent — "tell me about
the longest available stay per arrival, not every sub-length" — is preserved;
only the hard-coded 1-vs-2 assumption is removed.

### Explosion guard — warn, do not cap

No hard limit on enumerated windows. When a single pattern expands beyond a
threshold (planning to pick a concrete number, order of ~10), emit a warning via
the existing progress/log side-channel so the user notices a runaway span. The
search still runs.

### Unchanged contracts

`WeekendMatch` keeps its `start_date` / `end_date` / `nights` fields; `end_date`
remains the checkout day per the recent convention fix (`end = start + nights`).
Horizon, drive-time filtering, allowlist pre-filtering, rest-day suppression,
and the booking-URL builder are untouched and operate per emitted window.

## Testing Decisions

Good tests here assert **external behaviour at the seam**, not internal loop
mechanics: given a profile and a clock, what set of `(start_date, nights)`
windows comes out, and which matches survive dedup. No test should reach into
the enumeration's offset arithmetic.

- **`parse_pattern`** (`tests/test_profile.py::TestParsePattern`, existing prior
  art): add cases for `fri-mon:2-3` parsing, bare-pattern `min=max=span_nights`
  equivalence, and each new validation error (`min<1`, `min>max`, `max>span`).
- **`expand_windows`** (`tests/test_search.py`, alongside
  `TestExpandWindowsMinStart`): assert the exact emitted window set for a ranged
  pattern over a known horizon and fixed `today`; assert a bare pattern emits the
  same windows as before the change (regression lock); assert `min_start` /
  `max_start` / past-date guards still filter emitted starts.
- **Prefer-longest dedup** (`tests/test_search.py::TestSearchRunWithAllowed`
  style, driving `run()` with the fake `_AvailabilityApi`): when a fake reports
  availability for both a 2- and 3-night stay from the same Friday, assert only
  the 3-night `WeekendMatch` is yielded; when only the shorter stay is available,
  assert it still surfaces.
- **Backward-compat integration**: a profile containing only `["fri-sun"]`
  yields the identical matches it does today (guards the migration promise).

Use the existing fakes (`FakeApi`, `_AvailabilityApi`, `_make_profile`) and the
injected clock; do not add new test infrastructure.

## Out of Scope

- Calendar-date spans (e.g. "any 2 nights between Jul 1 and Jul 10"). This PRD
  is weekday-anchored spans only.
- Wrap-around spans crossing the week boundary (e.g. `sat-mon` as a literal
  Sat→Mon **of the same weekend** is fine; `sun-sat` wrapping is still rejected).
- A hard cap / rejection on window explosion — warning only.
- Changing notification, booking-URL, or `WeekendMatch` schema.
- Per-pattern overrides of horizon, drive-time, or rest-day settings.

## Further Notes

The backward-compatibility story is the load-bearing design choice: because a
bare span collapses to `min=max=span_nights`, the entire feature is additive and
no existing `profile.json` changes. Recommend shipping with the explosion-
warning threshold conservative, then loosening once real usage is observed.

The recent night-count convention fix (`end_date` = checkout day, `nights = end
- start`, fri-sun = 2 nights) is assumed throughout; enumeration must not
reintroduce the old off-by-one.
result: Flexible-days search feature shipped across 4 subtasks. parse_pattern accepts an optional :min-max suffix (1dd08bd): bare 'fri-sun' backwards-compat; :min-max returns (start_weekday, span_nights, min_nights, max_nights); validation: unknown day, same-day, min<1, min>max, max>span, malformed suffix; week-wrap >5 nights rejected. expand_windows enumeration (4b50394): full offset×length enumeration per PRD, guards on emitted start. Prefer-longest dedup (d44d07d): general 'skip shorter wholly-contained in accepted longer' replacing 1-vs-2 hard-coded suppression. Explosion warn + bcompat (ee81682): _EXPLOSION_THRESHOLD=10 per-pattern warning via progress callback; bare ['fri-sun'] regression-locked against ['fri-sun:2-2']. Total: 171 tests passing (up from 30 baseline), 4 commits, 2 review cycles with M3 (1 fix round for wrap-around rule). PRD acceptance criteria met: bare-pattern backward-compat, 2-3 night flexibility, prefer-longest per arrival, fail-fast on bad patterns, warning on explosion, all 13 user stories implementable via the new shape.

name: [L] multi-profile
description:
Support multiple search profiles. Each profile has its own parks, patterns, and telegram notification targets. The crawl process is optimized: overlapping park watches across profiles trigger a single API crawl, with results fanned out to all interested profiles. Profiles are stored in the SQLite DB (state.db), not in profile.json. A CLI will manage profile CRUD (create, list, edit, delete, switch). Current state: single Profile singleton loaded from ~/.campcli/profile.json.
Grilled: yes
PRD: .scratch/multi-profile/PRD.md
Base: f7ef965c33e88af282af316f00bf38ec0888b119

Commits: 913ab10, 2be487d, aee30ce, dfbb679, 92158a6. Cumulative: 41 files, +3382/-1360. 225 tests pass. archived_children.name: [M] multi-profile-poller-and-migration
archived_children.description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Refactor the Poller and daemon to support multiple profiles with crawl dedup. Add profile.json → DB migration.

Poller refactor:
- Load all enabled profiles via ProfileRepo.list_enabled()
- Collect unique park_id, map_id pairs across all profiles (resolve park/map queries against BC Parks catalog at start of each poll cycle)
- For each unique pair, sequentially: call BCParksApi.map_availability() once, then for each profile watching this park: run pattern explosion + availability matching + prefer-longest dedup. If matches found, notify via Telegram immediately (per-profile tg_allowed_ids), then proceed to next profile
- After all profiles for this park are processed, proceed to next park
- SearchNotifier is created per profile (each gets its own rest_days and drive_times config)

Daemon refactor:
- daemon.py loads ProfileRepo and passes list of enabled profiles to Poller
- Removes load_profile() call (no more profile.json)

Migration:
- On Poller startup (or first profile command), if profile.json exists and no profiles in DB: read profile.json, create a profile named 'default' with all fields populated from the JSON, delete profile.json
- This is a function called at daemon startup and before any profile command

The old application/profile.py load_profile and _make_default_profile are removed.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Poller.run_search_once() uses ProfileRepo.list_enabled() instead of a single Profile
- [ ] Unique park grouping: 2 profiles watching same park → API called once
- [ ] Sequential processing: park A → notify profiles → park B
- [ ] Per-profile notification: each profile's tg_allowed_ids receives only its matches
- [ ] SearchNotifier created per profile with profile-specific rest_days and drive_times
- [ ] Daemon loads profiles from ProfileRepo, not profile.json
- [ ] profile.json migration: exists + no DB profiles → create 'default' profile, delete JSON
- [ ] Migration called on daemon startup
- [ ] Old load_profile / _make_default_profile removed
- [ ] Unit tests: 2 profiles same park (dedup), 2 profiles different parks (no dedup), disabled profile skipped, empty profile list
- [ ] Integration test: daemon starts, migrates profile.json, polls correctly

## Blocked by
Profile children: 7e85bx3w

Poller.run_search_once() now: 1) loads enabled profiles from DB, 2) resolves park queries against catalog, 3) groups by unique (park_id, map_id) for API dedup, 4) for each pair calls map_availability once and fans out to all interested profiles with their per-profile tg_allowed_ids and rest_days, 5) moves to next park. name: [S] prefer-longest dedup
description: search.py run(): replace 1-vs-2 hard-coded suppression with generalised prefer-longest dedup. Per map, sort candidate windows by start_date asc then nights desc; track which (start_date) nights are already covered by an accepted longer stay; skip a shorter window whose nights are wholly contained in an already-accepted stay from same/earlier overlapping start. Intent preserved: longest available stay per arrival, not every sub-length. Tests (TestSearchRunWithAllowed style, fake _AvailabilityApi): when fake reports 2n+3n from same Friday, only 3n WeekendMatch is yielded; when only shorter is available, it still surfaces. Parent PRD: mq9caivh.
result: Replaced 1-vs-2 hard-coded suppression in src/campcli/application/search.py::run with general prefer-longest dedup per PRD: sort windows by (start asc, nights desc), accept first (gated on availability), skip any candidate whose range is wholly inside an already-accepted longer stay via a_start <= s AND s + n <= a_start + a_nights. Worked example (fri-mon:2-3 anchor Fri 06-13): windows sorted (06-13,3), (06-13,2), (06-14,2) — 3n accepted first, 2n windows both covered. 5 new TestSearchRunWithAllowed cases in tests/test_search.py covering: 3n-covers-2n, only-2n-still-surfaces, disjoint-anchor-both-yield, 1n-suppression regression, full 1-vs-2-vs-3 chain. Verification: 167 passed, 0 failed, 0 warnings, 0 xfailed in 1.19s. Review: cycle 1 PASS, 1 minor (comment wording) fixed inline. Commit: d44d07d.

name: [M] core-not-interested-repo-and-filter
description:
## Parent
[M] mark-not-interested

## What to build
Create the profile_not_interested and sent_notifications tables, the NotInterestedRepo protocol, and SqliteStore implementation. Integrate filtering into SearchNotifier so WeekendMatches matching a NotInterested entry are silently skipped. Change send_to() to return message_id for sent-message tracking.

Schema:
- profile_not_interested (id, profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end, UNIQUE on composite)
- sent_notifications (message_id PK, profile_id, park_id, date_start, date_end)

NotInterestedRepo protocol (domain/ports.py): add, remove, list_for, load_skip_set, record_sent, lookup_sent.

SqliteStore implements NotInterestedRepo with in-memory SQLite support. sent_notifications purge on daemon startup (rows older than 90 days).

SearchNotifier receives NotInterestedRepo via constructor. At start_poll(): calls load_skip_set(profile_id). In notify(): before NotificationPolicy.decide(), checks skip set and drops match if present. After successful send_to(): calls record_sent().

send_to() return type changes from None → int (Telegram message_id).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] profile_not_interested table created with UNIQUE constraint and CASCADE delete
- [ ] sent_notifications table created with message_id PK and CASCADE delete
- [ ] NotInterestedRepo protocol in domain/ports.py with all 6 methods
- [ ] SqliteStore implements all NotInterestedRepo methods
- [ ] sent_notifications purge on daemon startup (TTL 90 days)
- [ ] SearchNotifier accepts NotInterestedRepo via constructor
- [ ] SearchNotifier.start_poll() loads skip set for the profile
- [ ] SearchNotifier.notify() skips matches found in skip set
- [ ] SearchNotifier.notify() records sent messages after send_to() success
- [ ] send_to() returns message_id (int)
- [ ] Unit tests: repo CRUD with in-memory SQLite
- [ ] Unit tests: SearchNotifier filtering with mock repo
- [ ] Unit tests: cascade delete (profile → not_interested + sent_notifications)

## Blocked by

name: [M] telegram-not-interested-handler
description:
## Parent
[M] mark-not-interested

## What to build
Add the /not-interested Telegram reply command. Extend TelegramUpdate with reply_to_message_id, parse it in poll_updates(), handle the command in command_router.dispatch(), and add a footer to notification messages teaching users about the command.

Handler flow:
1. Extract reply_to_message_id from TelegramUpdate
2. Look up sent_notifications via NotInterestedRepo.lookup_sent()
3. If no match → reply 'Could not find the notification for this message.'
4. Verify from_id is in profile's tg_allowed_ids
5. Insert into profile_not_interested
6. Reply confirming: 'NotInterested recorded: <Park> <dates>'

Standalone /not-interested (no reply context) → 'Reply this command to a notification message.'

/not-interested is NOT registered as a bot command (reply-only).

render_match_message() adds footer: 'Reply /not-interested to stop seeing this park for these dates.'

- [ ] TelegramUpdate.reply_to_message_id field added (int | None)
- [ ] poll_updates() extracts reply_to_message.message_id from raw JSON
- [ ] dispatch() handles /not-interested when reply_to_message_id is present
- [ ] Standalone /not-interested returns guidance message
- [ ] Lookup failure returns clear error
